paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: By the year 2050, nearly 80% of the Earth's population will live in urban centres. Applying the most conservative estimates to current demographic trends, the human population will increase by about three billion people by then. An estimated 109 hectares of new land (about 20% larger than Brazil) will be needed to grow enough food to feed them, if traditional farming methods continue as they are practised today. At present, throughout the world, over 80% of the land that is suitable for raising crops is in use. Historically, some 15% of that has been laid waste by poor management practices. What can be done to ensure enough food for the world's population to live on?

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The concept of indoor farming is not new, since hothouse production of tomatoes and other produce has been in vogue for some time. What is new is the urgent need to scale up this technology to accommodate another three billion people. Many believe an entirely new approach to indoor farming is required, employing cutting-edge technologies. One such proposal is for the 'Vertical Farm'. The concept is of multi-storey buildings in which food crops are grown in environmentally controlled conditions. Situated in the heart of urban centres, they would drastically reduce the amount of transportation required to bring food to consumers. Vertical farms would need to be efficient, cheap to construct and safe to operate. If successfully implemented, proponents claim, vertical farms offer the promise of urban renewal, sustainable production of a safe and varied food supply (through year-round production of all crops), and the eventual repair of ecosystems that have been sacrificed for horizontal farming.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: It took humans 10,000 years to learn how to grow most of the crops we now take for granted. Along the way we despoiled most of the land we worked, often turning verdant, natural ecozones into semi-arid deserts. Within that same time frame, we evolved into an urban species, in which 60% of the human population now lives vertically in cities. This means that, for the majority, we humans have shelter from the elements, yet we subject our food-bearing plants to the rigours of the great outdoors and can do no more than hope for a good weather year. However, more often than not now, due to a rapidly changing climate that is not what happens. Massive floods, long droughts, hurricanes and severe monsoons take their toll each year, destroying millions of tons of valuable crops.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The supporters of vertical farming claim many potential advantages for the system. For instance, crops would be produced all year round, as they would be kept in artificially controlled, optimum growing conditions. There would be no weather-related crop failures due to droughts, floods or pests. All the food could be grown organically, eliminating the need for herbicides, pesticides and fertilisers. The system would greatly reduce the incidence of many infectious diseases that are acquired at the agricultural interface. Although the system would consume energy, it would return energy to the grid via methane generation from composting non-edible parts of plants. It would also dramatically reduce fossil fuel use, by cutting out the need for tractors, ploughs and shipping.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: A major drawback of vertical farming, however, is that the plants would require artificial light. Without it, those plants nearest the windows would be exposed to more sunlight and grow more quickly, reducing the efficiency of the system. Single-storey greenhouses have the benefit of natural overhead light: even so, many still need artificial lighting. A multi-storey facility with no natural overhead light would require far more. Generating enough light could be prohibitively expensive, unless cheap, renewable energy is available, and this appears to be rather a future aspiration than a likelihood for the near future.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: One variation on vertical farming that has been developed is to grow plants in stacked trays that move on rails. Moving the trays allows the plants to get enough sunlight. This system is already in operation, and works well within a single-storey greenhouse with light reaching it from above: it is not certain, however, that it can be made to work without that overhead natural light.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Vertical farming is an attempt to address the undoubted problems that we face in producing enough food for a growing population. At the moment, though, more needs to be done to reduce the detrimental impact it would have on the environment, particularly as regards the use of energy. While it is possible that much of our food will be grown in skyscrapers in future, most experts currently believe it is far more likely that we will simply use the space available on urban rooftops.

groups.desc:
Questions 8-13

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: Methods for predicting the Earth's population have recently changed.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“methods”、“predicting”和“recently changed”在原文中分别对应“estimates”、“demographic trends”，但没有找到“recently changed”的对应信息。原文中提到的是当前的人口趋势和估计方法，但并没有提到这些方法是否最近发生了变化。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Applying the most conservative estimates to current demographic trends, the human population will increase by about three billion people by then.
central_sentences.translation: 根据当前的人口趋势，应用最保守的估计，到2050年人口将增加约30亿人。

questions.analysis: 题干中的“depend on the season”意思是“依赖于季节”，而原文中的“all year round”意思是“全年”。这两个表达是相反的，说明垂直农场中的作物生产不依赖于季节，而是全年进行。因此，题干信息与原文信息相反，正确答案是FALSE。

questions.title: Vertical farming will make plants less likely to be affected by infectious diseases.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Vertical farming will make plants less likely to be affected by infectious diseases.”意思是垂直农业会使植物不太可能受到传染病的影响。原文中提到“The system would greatly reduce the incidence of many infectious diseases that are acquired at the agricultural interface.”，意思是该系统将大大减少在农业界面上获得的许多传染病的发生率。这里的“greatly reduce”与“less likely”是同义表达，因此可以确定答案是TRUE。

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The Falkirk Wheel in Scotland is the world's first and only rotating boat lift. Opened in 2002, it is central to the ambitious £84.5m Millennium Link project to restore navigability across Scotland by reconnecting the historic waterways of the Forth & Clyde and Union Canals.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The major challenge of the project lay in the fact that the Forth & Clyde Canal is situated 35 metres below the level of the Union Canal. Historically, the two canals had been joined near the town of Falkirk by a sequence of 11 locks - enclosed sections of canal in which the water level could be raised or lowered - that stepped down across a distance of 1.5 km. This had been dismantled in 1933, thereby breaking the link. When the project was launched in 1994, the British Waterways authority were keen to create a dramatic twenty-first-century landmark which would not only be a fitting commemoration of the Millennium, but also a lasting symbol of the economic regeneration of the region.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Numerous ideas were submitted for the project, including concepts ranging from rolling eggs to tilting tanks, from giant seesaws to overhead monorails. The eventual winner was a plan for the huge rotating steel boat lift which was to become The Falkirk Wheel. The unique shape of the structure is claimed to have been inspired by various sources, both manmade and natural, most notably a Celtic double-headed axe, but also the vast turning propeller of a ship, the ribcage of a whale or the spine of a fish.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The various parts of The Falkirk Wheel were all constructed and assembled, like one giant toy building set, at Butterley Engineering's Steelworks in Derbyshire, some 400 km from Falkirk. A team there carefully assembled the 1,200 tonnes of steel, painstakingly fitting the pieces together to an accuracy of just 10 mm to ensure a perfect final fit. In the summer of 2001, the structure was then dismantled and transported on 35 lorries to Falkirk, before all being bolted back together again on the ground, and finally lifted into position in five large sections by crane. The Wheel would need to withstand immense and constantly changing stresses as it rotated, so to make the structure more robust, the steel sections were bolted rather than welded together. Over 45,000 bolt holes were matched with their bolts, and each bolt was hand-tightened.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The Wheel consists of two sets of opposing axe-shaped arms, attached about 25 metres apart to a fixed central spine. Two diametrically opposed water-filled 'gondolas', each with a capacity of 360,000 litres, are fitted between the ends of the arms. These gondolas always weigh the same, whether or not they are carrying boats. This is because, according to Archimedes' principle of displacement, floating objects displace their own weight in water. So when a boat enters a gondola, the amount of water leaving the gondola weighs exactly the same as the boat. This keeps the Wheel balanced and so, despite its enormous mass, it rotates through 180° in five and a half minutes while using very little power. It takes just 1.5 kilowatt-hours (5.4 MJ) of energy to rotate the Wheel - roughly the same as boiling eight small domestic kettles of water.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Boats needing to be lifted up enter the canal basin at the level of the Forth & Clyde Canal and then enter the lower gondola of the Wheel. Two hydraulic steel gates are raised, so as to seal the gondola off from the water in the canal basin. The water between the gates is then pumped out. A hydraulic clamp, which prevents the arms of the Wheel moving while the gondola is docked, is removed, allowing the Wheel to turn. In the central machine room an array of ten hydraulic motors then begins to rotate the central axle. The axle connects to the outer arms of the Wheel, which begin to rotate at a speed of 1/8 of a revolution per minute. As the wheel rotates, the gondolas are kept in the upright position by a simple gearing system. Two eight-metre-wide cogs orbit a fixed inner cog of the same width, connected by two smaller cogs travelling in the opposite direction to the outer cogs - so ensuring that the gondolas always remain level. When the gondola reaches the top, the boat passes straight onto the aqueduct situated 24 metres above the canal basin.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The remaining 11 metres of lift needed to reach the Union Canal is achieved by means of a pair of locks. The Wheel could not be constructed to elevate boats over the full 35-metre difference between the two canals, owing to the presence of the historically important Antonine Wall, which was built by the Romans in the second century AD. Boats travel under this wall via a tunnel, then through the locks, and finally on to the Union Canal.

groups.desc:
Questions 14-19

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 2?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: The Falkirk Wheel has linked the Forth & Clyde Canal with the Union Canal for the first time in their history.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“for the first time in their history”表示这是第一次，而原文中明确提到“Historically, the two canals had been joined”，即历史上这两条运河曾经连接过。因此，题干中的说法是错误的，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Historically, the two canals had been joined near the town of Falkirk by a sequence of 11 locks - enclosed sections of canal in which the water level could be raised or lowered - that stepped down across a distance of 1.5 km. This had been dismantled in 1933, thereby breaking the link.
central_sentences.translation: 历史上，这两条运河曾在福克克镇附近通过一系列11个水闸连接起来 - 这些水闸是封闭的运河段，可以升高或降低水位 - 跨越1.5公里的距离逐步下降。这个连接在1933年被拆除，因此中断了两条运河之间的联系。

target_sen.target_sen: Historically, the two canals had been joined near the town of Falkirk by a sequence of 11 locks - enclosed sections of canal in which the water level could be raised or lowered - that stepped down across a distance of 1.5 km.

Historically, the two canals had been joined near the town of Falkirk by a sequence of 11 locks - enclosed sections of canal in which the water level could be raised or lowered - that stepped down across a distance of 1.5 km.
The major challenge of the project lay in the fact that the Forth & Clyde Canal is situated 35 metres below the level of the Union Canal.
The major challenge of the project lay in the fact that the Forth & Clyde Canal is situated 35 metres below the level of the Union Canal.

questions.title: There was some opposition to the design of the Falkirk Wheel at first.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”，因为文章中没有提到任何关于Falkirk Wheel设计最初有反对意见的信息。通过关键词的对比和寻找，学生可以发现文章内容与题干内容没有直接对应的反对描述，从而得出正确答案。在做类似题目时，学生需要仔细寻找文章中是否有相关信息，如果没有明确提到，则选择“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.reason: 原文提到多种设计方案，但未提反对意见。
central_sentences.analysis: 文中未提及是否有人反对设计。

questions.title: The Falkirk Wheel was initially put together at the location where its components were manufactured.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "initially put together" 对应原文中的 "constructed and assembled"，而 "location where its components were manufactured" 对应 "Butterley Engineering's Steelworks"。原文明确提到Falkirk Wheel的各个部分是在制造它们的地方（Butterley Engineering's Steelworks）组装的，因此答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: The various parts of The Falkirk Wheel were all constructed and assembled, like one giant toy building set, at Butterley Engineering's Steelworks in Derbyshire, some 400 km from Falkirk.
central_sentences.translation: 法尔柯克轮的各个部件都像一个巨大的玩具套装一样在德比郡巴特利工程钢铁厂建造和组装，该工厂距离法尔柯克约400公里。

target_sen.target_sen: The various parts of The Falkirk Wheel were all constructed and assembled, like one giant toy building set, at Butterley Engineering's Steelworks in Derbyshire, some 400 km from Falkirk.
The various parts of The Falkirk Wheel were all constructed and assembled, like one giant toy building set, at Butterley Engineering's Steelworks in Derbyshire, some 400 km from Falkirk.

locating_words.locating_word: location where its components were manufactured

questions.title: The Falkirk Wheel is the only boat lift in the world which has steel sections bolted together by hand.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词包括“only”和“steel sections bolted together by hand”。在原文中，我们找到了与“steel sections bolted together by hand”对应的同义替换词“each bolt was hand-tightened”和“bolted back together again on the ground”。然而，题干中的“only”这一限定词在原文中并没有提到，也就是说，文章并没有说明Falkirk Wheel是世界上唯一一个使用这种技术的升船机。因此，题干中的信息在文章中并没有明确提到，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: final fit. In the summer of 2001, the structure was then dismantled and transported on 35 lorries to Falkirk, before all being bolted back together again on the ground, and finally lifted into position in five large sections by crane. The Wheel would need to withstand immense and constantly changing stresses
central_sentences.translation: 2001年夏天，整个结构被拆解并用35辆卡车运送到法尔柯克，然后在地面上重新用螺栓连接在一起，最后用起重机分五大部分吊装到位。...超过45,000个螺栓孔与螺栓匹配，每个螺栓都是手工拧紧的。

target_sen.target_sen: The various parts of The Falkirk Wheel were all constructed and assembled, like one giant toy building set, at Butterley Engineering's Steelworks in Derbyshire, some 400 km from Falkirk.

In the summer of 2001, the structure was then dismantled and transported on 35 lorries to Falkirk, before all being bolted back together again on the ground, and finally lifted into position in five large sections by crane.
Over 45,000 bolt holes were matched with their bolts, and each bolt was hand-tightened.

questions.title: The weight of the gondolas varies according to the size of boat being carried.
questions.analysis: 题干中的陈述是“吊舱的重量根据所载船只的大小而变化”，而原文明确指出“这些吊舱的重量始终相同，无论它们是否载有船只”。这直接反驳了题干的陈述，因此正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: These gondolas always weigh the same, whether or not they are carrying boats.
central_sentences.translation: 这些吊舱的重量始终相同，无论它们是否载有船只。

target_sen.target_sen: These gondolas always weigh the same, whether or not they are carrying boats.
This is because, according to Archimedes' principle of displacement, floating objects displace their own weight in water.
So when a boat enters a gondola, the amount of water leaving the gondola weighs exactly the same as the boat.
Two diametrically opposed water-filled 'gondolas', each with a capacity of 360,000 litres, are fitted between the ends of the arms.
These gondolas always weigh the same, whether or not they are carrying boats.
These gondolas always weigh the same, whether or not they are carrying boats.

questions.title: The construction of the Falkirk Wheel site took into account the presence of a nearby ancient monument.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“福尔柯克轮的建设考虑到了附近古迹的存在”在原文中得到了明确的对应。原文提到，由于安东尼墙的存在，福尔柯克轮不能完全提升两条运河之间的高度差，这表明建设过程中确实考虑到了古迹的存在。因此，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: The Wheel could not be constructed to elevate boats over the full 35-metre difference between the two canals, owing to the presence of the historically important Antonine Wall, which was built by the Romans in the second century AD.
central_sentences.translation: 由于历史上重要的安东尼墙的存在，轮船不能完全提升两条运河之间的35米高度差。安东尼墙是罗马人在公元二世纪修建的。

target_sen.target_sen: The Wheel could not be constructed to elevate boats over the full 35-metre difference between the two canals, owing to the presence of the historically important Antonine Wall, which was built by the Romans in the second century AD.

The Wheel could not be constructed to elevate boats over the full 35-metre difference between the two canals, owing to the presence of the historically important Antonine Wall, which was built by the Romans in the second century AD.

groups.title: How a boat is lifted on the Falkirk Wheel
groups.desc:
Questions 20-26

Label the diagram below.
Write ONE WORD from the text in each gap.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "shut out water" 和原文中的 "seal the gondola off from the water" 是同义表达，指的是阻挡水的装置。题干中的 "canal basin" 在原文中也直接出现。题干中的 "lifted" 对应原文中的 "raised"，都是指抬起的动作。通过这些线索词，可以定位到原文中的答案句 "Two hydraulic steel gates are raised, so as to seal the gondola off from the water in the canal basin."，由此可以确定答案是 "gates"。

central_sentences.sentence: Two hydraulic steel gates are raised, so as to seal the gondola off from the water in the canal basin.
central_sentences.translation: 两扇液压钢闸门被抬起，以便将水从运河水池中隔离开来。

syn_ary.synonym: seal the gondola off from the water

target_sen.target_sen: Two hydraulic steel gates are raised, so as to seal the gondola off from the water in the canal basin.
Boats needing to be lifted up enter the canal basin at the level of the Forth & Clyde Canal and then enter the lower gondola of the Wheel.
Two hydraulic steel gates are raised, so as to seal the gondola off from the water in the canal basin.
When the gondola reaches the top, the boat passes straight onto the aqueduct situated 24 metres above the canal basin.
Two hydraulic steel gates are raised, so as to seal the gondola off from the water in the canal basin.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "is taken out" 和 "enabling Wheel to rotate" 对应文章中的 "is removed" 和 "allowing the Wheel to turn"。这些表达的意思是相同的，都是指某个东西被移除后使轮子可以转动。文章中提到 "A hydraulic clamp... is removed"，说明被移除的东西是 "hydraulic clamp"。因此，正确答案是 "clamp"。

central_sentences.sentence: A hydraulic clamp, which prevents the arms of the Wheel moving while the gondola is docked, is removed, allowing the Wheel to turn.
central_sentences.translation: 一个液压夹具，在船舱停靠时防止轮子的臂移动，被移除，使轮子可以转动。

target_sen.target_sen: A hydraulic clamp, which prevents the arms of the Wheel moving while the gondola is docked, is removed, allowing the Wheel to turn.
A hydraulic clamp, which prevents the arms of the Wheel moving while the gondola is docked, is removed, allowing the Wheel to turn.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“Hydraulic motors drive 22 ________”意思是“液压马达驱动22 ________”。在文章中找到对应的句子：“In the central machine room an array of ten hydraulic motors then begins to rotate the central axle.” 这里的“hydraulic motors”对应题干中的“Hydraulic motors”，“rotate”对应“drive”，“central axle”对应22的空格。因此，最直接的同义表达是“axle”。学生可能会错填“arms”或“gondolas”，因为这些词在文章中也出现了，并且与Falkirk Wheel的结构有关。但“arms”是被“axle”驱动的，而不是直接被“hydraulic motors”驱动的；“gondolas”也是被“arms”带动的，而不是直接被“hydraulic motors”驱动的。因此，正确答案是“axle”。

central_sentences.sentence: In the central machine room an array of ten hydraulic motors then begins to rotate the central axle.
central_sentences.translation: 在中央机房中，十个液压马达开始旋转中央轴。

target_sen.target_sen: In the central machine room an array of ten hydraulic motors then begins to rotate the central axle.
In the central machine room an array of ten hydraulic motors then begins to rotate the central axle.
The axle connects to the outer arms of the Wheel, which begin to rotate at a speed of 1/8 of a revolution per minute.
As the wheel rotates, the gondolas are kept in the upright position by a simple gearing system.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“ensures boat keeps upright”在原文中对应的是“kept in the upright position”，而“range of different-sized”对应的是“different-sized”。原文提到“Two eight-metre-wide cogs orbit a fixed inner cog of the same width, connected by two smaller cogs travelling in the opposite direction to the outer cogs - so ensuring that the gondolas always remain level.”，这里明确指出是“cogs”确保了吊舱保持水平。因此，答案是“cogs”。

central_sentences.sentence: As the wheel rotates, the gondolas are kept in the upright position by a simple gearing system. Two eight-metre-wide cogs orbit a fixed inner cog of the same width, connected by two smaller cogs travelling in the opposite direction to the outer cogs - so ensuring that the gondolas always remain level.
central_sentences.translation: 当轮子旋转时，吊舱通过一个简单的齿轮系统保持直立。两个八米宽的齿轮围绕一个固定的内齿轮旋转，连接着两个朝相反方向移动的小齿轮——这样确保吊舱始终保持水平。

target_sen.target_sen: As the wheel rotates, the gondolas are kept in the upright position by a simple gearing system.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "moves directly onto" 和原文中的 "passes straight onto" 是同义表达，确认了对应关系。题干中的 "top Wheel" 和原文中的 "reaches the top" 也是同义表达。通过这些线索词，可以定位到原文中的答案句。原文中明确提到 "the boat passes straight onto the aqueduct"，因此答案是 "aqueduct"。学生需要注意的是，"canal" 和 "lock" 虽然也是水道的一部分，但它们并不符合题干中描述的船到达轮顶后的移动。

central_sentences.sentence: When the gondola reaches the top, the boat passes straight onto the aqueduct situated 24 metres above the canal basin.
central_sentences.translation: 当吊舱到达顶部时，船直接进入位于运河盆地上方24米处的水道桥。

target_sen.target_sen: When the gondola reaches the top, the boat passes straight onto the aqueduct situated 24 metres above the canal basin.
When the gondola reaches the top, the boat passes straight onto the aqueduct situated 24 metres above the canal basin.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“beneath Roman _______”在文章中对应的是“under this wall via a tunnel”。这里的“wall”就是答案。题干明确提到的是“Roman”相关的结构，而不是“tunnel”或“locks”。通过关键词定位和上下文理解，即使词汇量有限的学生也可以找到正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The Wheel could not be constructed to elevate boats over the full 35-metre difference between the two canals, owing to the presence of the historically important Antonine Wall, which was built by the Romans in the second century AD. Boats travel under this wall via a tunnel, then through the locks, and finally on to the Union Canal.
central_sentences.translation: 由于历史上重要的安东尼墙的存在，轮子无法建造以提升船只超过两条运河之间的35米高度差。这堵墙是由罗马人在公元二世纪建造的。船只通过这堵墙下的隧道，然后通过水闸，最后到达联合运河。

target_sen.target_sen: The Wheel could not be constructed to elevate boats over the full 35-metre difference between the two canals, owing to the presence of the historically important Antonine Wall, which was built by the Romans in the second century AD.
Boats travel under this wall via a tunnel, then through the locks, and finally on to the Union Canal.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "raise boat 11 m to level of Union Canal" 对应文章中的 "11 metres of lift needed to reach the Union Canal"。题干中的 "raise" 对应文章中的 "lift"，"11 m" 对应 "11 metres"，"level of Union Canal" 对应 "reach the Union Canal"。由此可以确认答案所在的句子为 "The remaining 11 metres of lift needed to reach the Union Canal is achieved by means of a pair of locks."。因此，答案是 "locks"。

central_sentences.sentence: The remaining 11 metres of lift needed to reach the Union Canal is achieved by means of a pair of locks.
central_sentences.translation: 剩余的11米提升需要通过一对船闸来实现，以到达联合运河。

target_sen.target_sen: The remaining 11 metres of lift needed to reach the Union Canal is achieved by means of a pair of locks.
The remaining 11 metres of lift needed to reach the Union Canal is achieved by means of a pair of locks.
The remaining 11 metres of lift needed to reach the Union Canal is achieved by means of a pair of locks.

content.title: Reducing the Effects of Climate Change
content.summary:
{'A': '由于我们对化石燃料的依赖以及已经释放到大气中的二氧化碳的数量，许多专家认为全球变暖是不可避免的，他们认为我们能做的最好的事情就是将其保持在一个合理的水平，而目前唯一严肃的选择就是减少碳排放。然而，虽然有一些国家在这方面取得了重大进展，但大多数国家在阻止增长速度甚至逆转增长方面都遇到了很大困难。因此，越来越多的科学家开始探索地球工程的替代方案，这个术语通常指的是有意识地对环境进行大规模的操控。根据支持者的说法，地球工程就像备用发电机一样：如果A计划-减少对化石燃料的依赖-失败了，我们需要B计划，采用宏大的方案来减缓或逆转全球变暖的过程。', 'B': '地球工程学已被证明在小范围内是有效的，例如莫斯科的五一游行活动中使用干冰、碘化银和水泥粉末来驱散云层。现在提出的许多方案都是相反的，旨在减少阳光照射地球的量。亚利桑那大学的罗杰·安吉尔教授提出的最引人注目的想法是利用多达16万亿个质量约为一克的微型航天器，在距离地球150万公里的轨道上形成一个透明的、折射阳光的遮阳伞。安吉尔认为，这可以减少达到地球的光线量达到百分之二。

简短总结：地球工程学已被证明在小范围内是有效的，现在有一种想法是利用微型航天器形成一个透明的、折射阳光的遮阳伞来减少阳光照射地球的量。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了大部分地球工程项目都致力于实现地球的整体降温，但也有一些项目专注于逆转极地的融化，特别是北极，通过补充高纬度地区的冰层和冻水，增加反射光线，从而减少海洋和大气的变暖。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了一些科学家提出的在北极平流层释放气溶胶喷雾的概念，以达到全球变暗的效果，并模仿历史上火山爆发的情况来降低全球温度。同时，还提到了保护格陵兰冰盖的方法，包括使用高张力电缆阻止冰山进入海洋，以及在俄罗斯北极地区种植白桦树和改变河流路径来减缓变暖的计划。', 'E': '这段话主要讲了科学家对地球工程的谨慎态度以及他们对可再生能源的重要性的认识，同时也提到了如果停止地球工程，地球将很快恢复到之前的状态，这是一个令人担忧的问题。

简短总结：科学家对地球工程持谨慎态度，认为可再生能源是唯一的永久解决方案，同时停止地球工程可能会导致地球迅速恢复到之前的状态，这是一个令人担忧的问题。', 'F': '美国国家大气研究中心已经提出，向大气中注入硫磺可能会影响热带和南大洋地区的降雨模式。', 'G': '该段话主要讲述了地理工程项目存在的进一步风险是可能会“超调”，即全球温度可能会回到工业化前的水平，但极地仍然会比应有的温暖，而热带地区则会比工业化前更冷。为了避免这种情况，该项目需要以一半的强度运行，这进一步强调了作者认为最好的选择是避免完全依赖地理工程的观点。

简短总结：地理工程项目存在超调风险，最好的选择是避免完全依赖地理工程。', 'H': '科学界支持地理工程的主要原因是大多数研究人员对政治家达成并实施必要的碳减排措施的能力缺乏信心，即使是领先的保护组织也认为研究地理工程的潜力具有价值。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Such is our dependence on fossil fuels, and such is the volume of carbon dioxide already released into the atmosphere, that many experts agree that significant global warming is now inevitable. They believe that the best we can do is keep it at a reasonable level, and at present the only serious option for doing this is cutting back on our carbon emissions. But while a few countries are making major strides in this regard, the majority are having great difficulty even stemming the rate of increase, let alone reversing it. Consequently, an increasing number of scientists are beginning to explore the alternative of geo-engineering - a term which generally refers to the intentional large-scale manipulation of the environment. According to its proponents, geo-engineering is the equivalent of a backup generator: if Plan A - reducing our dependency on fossil fuels – fails, we require a Plan B, employing grand schemes to slow down or reverse the process of global warming.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Geo-engineering has been shown to work, at least on a small localised scale. For decades, May Day parades in Moscow have taken place under clear blue skies, aircraft having deposited dry ice, silver iodide and cement powder to disperse clouds. Many of the schemes now suggested look to do the opposite, and reduce the amount of sunlight reaching the planet. The most eye-catching idea of all is suggested by Professor Roger Angel of the University of Arizona. His scheme would employ up to 16 trillion minute spacecraft, each weighing about one gram, to form a transparent, sunlight-refracting sunshade in an orbit 1.5 million km above the Earth. This could, argues Angel, reduce the amount of light reaching the Earth by two per cent.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The majority of geo-engineering projects so far carried out - which include planting forests in deserts and depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae - have focused on achieving a general cooling of the Earth. But some look specifically at reversing the melting at the poles, particularly the Arctic. The reasoning is that if you replenish the ice sheets and frozen waters of the high latitudes, more light will be reflected back into space, so reducing the warming of the oceans and atmosphere.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The concept of releasing aerosol sprays into the stratosphere above the Arctic has been proposed by several scientists. This would involve using sulphur or hydrogen sulphide aerosols so that sulphur dioxide would form clouds, which would, in turn, lead to a global dimming. The idea is modelled on historic volcanic explosions, such as that of Mount Pinatubo in the Philippines in 1991, which led to a short-term cooling of global temperatures by 0.5 °C. Scientists have also scrutinised whether it's possible to preserve the ice sheets of Greenland with reinforced high-tension cables, preventing icebergs from moving into the sea. Meanwhile in the Russian Arctic, geo-engineering plans include the planting of millions of birch trees. Whereas the region's native evergreen pines shade the snow and absorb radiation, birches would shed their leaves in winter, thus enabling radiation to be reflected by the snow. Re-routing Russian rivers to increase cold water flow to ice-forming areas could also be used to slow down warming, say some climate scientists.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: But will such schemes ever be implemented? Generally speaking, those who are most cautious about geo-engineering are the scientists involved in the research. Angel says that his plan is 'no substitute for developing renewable energy: the only permanent solution'. And Dr Phil Rasch of the US-based Pacific Northwest National Laboratory is equally guarded about the role of geo-engineering: 'I think all of us agree that if we were to end geo-engineering on a given day, then the planet would return to its pre-engineered condition very rapidly, and probably within ten to twenty years. That's certainly something to worry about.'

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The US National Center for Atmospheric Research has already suggested that the proposal to inject sulphur into the atmosphere might affect rainfall patterns across the tropics and the Southern Ocean. 'Geo-engineering plans to inject stratospheric aerosols or to seed clouds would act to cool the planet, and act to increase the extent of sea ice,' says Rasch. 'But all the models suggest some impact on the distribution of precipitation.'

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: 'A further risk with geo-engineering projects is that you can "overshoot",' says Dr Dan Lunt, from the University of Bristol's School of Geophysical Sciences, who has studied the likely impacts of the sunshade and aerosol schemes on the climate. 'You may bring global temperatures back to pre-industrial levels, but the risk is that the poles will still be warmer than they should be and the tropics will be cooler than before industrialisation. To avoid such a scenario, Lunt says Angel's project would have to operate at half strength; all of which reinforces his view that the best option is to avoid the need for geo-engineering altogether.'

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The main reason why geo-engineering is supported by many in the scientific community is that most researchers have little faith in the ability of politicians to agree - and then bring in - the necessary carbon cuts. Even leading conservation organisations see the value of investigating the potential of geo-engineering. According to Dr Martin Sommerkorn, climate change advisor for the World Wildlife Fund's International Arctic Programme, 'Human-induced climate change has brought humanity to a position where we shouldn't exclude thinking thoroughly about this topic and its possibilities.'

groups.desc:
Questions 27-29
Which paragraph contains the following information?
Choose the correct answer.

questions.title: mention of a geo-engineering project based on an earlier natural phenomenon
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "mention of a geo-engineering project based on an earlier natural phenomenon" 直接对应段落 D 中提到的 "The idea is modelled on historic volcanic explosions, such as that of Mount Pinatubo in the Philippines in 1991"。这说明该地球工程项目是基于早期的自然现象（火山爆发）。因此，段落 D 是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The idea is modelled on historic volcanic explosions, such as that of Mount Pinatubo in the Philippines in 1991, which led to a short-term cooling of global temperatures by 0.5 °C.
central_sentences.translation: 这个想法是基于历史上的火山爆发而建模的，比如1991年菲律宾皮纳图博火山的爆发，它导致全球温度短期下降了0.5°C。

target_sen.target_sen: The idea is modelled on historic volcanic explosions, such as that of Mount Pinatubo in the Philippines in 1991, which led to a short-term cooling of global temperatures by 0.5 °C.

questions.title: an example of a successful use of geo-engineering
questions.analysis: 题干要求找到一个成功的地球工程应用实例。段落B明确提到“Geo-engineering has been shown to work, at least on a small localised scale”，并举例说明了莫斯科五一游行中通过飞机撒布干冰、碘化银和水泥粉来驱散云层的成功实例。这正是题干中所要求的“成功的地球工程应用实例”。

central_sentences.sentence: Geo-engineering has been shown to work, at least on a small localised scale. For decades, May Day parades in Moscow have taken place under clear blue skies, aircraft having deposited dry ice, silver iodide and cement powder to disperse clouds.
central_sentences.translation: 地球工程已被证明是有效的，至少在小范围内是如此。数十年来，莫斯科的五一游行一直在晴朗的蓝天下举行，飞机会撒布干冰、碘化银和水泥粉来驱散云层。

target_sen.target_sen: Geo-engineering has been shown to work, at least on a small localised scale.

syn_ary.synonym: For decades, May Day parades in Moscow

target_sen.target_sen: For decades, May Day parades in Moscow have taken place under clear blue skies, aircraft having deposited dry ice, silver iodide and cement powder to disperse clouds.

questions.title: a common definition of geo-engineering
questions.analysis: 题目要求找到地球工程的通用定义。段落A中明确给出了地球工程的定义：“geo-engineering - a term which generally refers to the intentional large-scale manipulation of the environment.” 这句话直接解释了什么是地球工程，因此是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: geo-engineering - a term which generally refers to the intentional large-scale manipulation of the environment. According
central_sentences.translation: 地球工程——这个术语通常指的是对环境的有意大规模操控。

target_sen.target_sen: Consequently, an increasing number of scientists are beginning to explore the alternative of geo-engineering - a term which generally refers to the intentional large-scale manipulation of the environment.

Consequently, an increasing number of scientists are beginning to explore the alternative of geo-engineering - a term which generally refers to the intentional large-scale manipulation of the environment.

According to its proponents, geo-engineering is the equivalent of a backup generator: if Plan A - reducing our dependency on fossil fuels – fails, we require a Plan B, employing grand schemes to slow down or reverse the process of global warming.

groups.title: GEO-ENGINEERING PROJECTS
groups.desc:
Questions 30-36
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD from the text in each gap.

table.title: GEO-ENGINEERING PROJECTS
content: put a large number of tiny spacecraft into orbit far above Earth
to create a $1137$that would reduce the amount of light reaching Earth
release aerosol sprays into the stratosphere
to create $1140$that would reduce the amount of light reaching Earth
to prevent icebergs moving into the sea
plant trees in Russian Arctic that would lose their leaves in winter
to bring more cold water into ice-forming areas

questions.analysis: 答案是 "sunshade"。题干中的 "tiny spacecraft" 对应原文中的 "minute spacecraft"，"reduce the amount of light" 对应原文中的 "reduce the amount of light"。原文中提到这些微小的航天器将形成一个 "sunshade"，其目的是减少到达地球的光量。因此，答案是 "sunshade"。

central_sentences.sentence: His scheme would employ up to 16 trillion minute spacecraft, each weighing about one gram, to form a transparent, sunlight-refracting sunshade in an orbit 1.5 million km above the Earth.
central_sentences.translation: 他的方案将使用多达16万亿个微小的航天器，每个重约一克，在距地球150万公里的轨道上形成一个透明的、可折射阳光的遮阳板。

target_sen.target_sen: His scheme would employ up to 16 trillion minute spacecraft, each weighing about one gram, to form a transparent, sunlight-refracting sunshade in an orbit 1.5 million km above the Earth.

syn_ary.synonym: reduce the amount of light reaching the Earth

target_sen.target_sen: This could, argues Angel, reduce the amount of light reaching the Earth by two per cent.

questions.analysis: 答案是“iron”，因为题干中的“place 【 】in the sea”与原文中的“depositing iron in the ocean”意思相同。题干中的“place”对应原文中的“depositing”，“in the sea”对应原文中的“in the ocean”。通过这些同义替换词，我们可以确定答案所在的句子，并找到正确答案“iron”。

central_sentences.sentence: The majority of geo-engineering projects so far carried out - which include planting forests in deserts and depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae - have focused on achieving a general cooling of the Earth.
central_sentences.translation: 到目前为止开展的大多数地球工程项目 - 包括在沙漠中种植森林和在海洋中沉积铁以刺激藻类生长 - 都集中在实现地球的整体降温。

target_sen.target_sen: The majority of geo-engineering projects so far carried out - which include planting forests in deserts and depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae - have focused on achieving a general cooling of the Earth.

The majority of geo-engineering projects so far carried out - which include planting forests in deserts and depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae - have focused on achieving a general cooling of the Earth.
The reasoning is that if you replenish the ice sheets and frozen waters of the high latitudes, more light will be reflected back into space, so reducing the warming of the oceans and atmosphere.

questions.analysis: 答案是“algae”，因为题干中的“place in the sea”对应文章中的“depositing in the ocean”，“to encourage ... to form”对应“stimulate the growth of”。文章明确提到“depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae”，即在海洋中沉积铁以刺激藻类的生长。因此，答案是“algae”。

central_sentences.sentence: The majority of geo-engineering projects so far carried out - which include planting forests in deserts and depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae - have focused on achieving a general cooling of the Earth.
central_sentences.translation: 到目前为止，已经开展的大多数地球工程项目——包括在沙漠中种植森林和在海洋中沉积铁以刺激藻类生长——都集中于实现地球的整体降温。

target_sen.target_sen: The majority of geo-engineering projects so far carried out - which include planting forests in deserts and depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae - have focused on achieving a general cooling of the Earth.

The majority of geo-engineering projects so far carried out - which include planting forests in deserts and depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae - have focused on achieving a general cooling of the Earth.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "clouds"。题目中的线索词 "release aerosol sprays" 在原文中对应 "releasing aerosol sprays"，而 "reduce the amount of light" 对应 "global dimming"。原文中提到 "sulphur dioxide would form clouds"，这些云会导致全球变暗，从而减少到达地球的光线。因此，答案是 "clouds"。

central_sentences.sentence: This would involve using sulphur or hydrogen sulphide aerosols so that sulphur dioxide would form clouds, which would, in turn, lead to a global dimming.
central_sentences.translation: 这将涉及使用硫或硫化氢气溶胶，使二氧化硫形成云，从而导致全球变暗。

syn_ary.synonym: releasing aerosol sprays into the stratosphere above the Arctic

target_sen.target_sen: The concept of releasing aerosol sprays into the stratosphere above the Arctic has been proposed by several scientists.

locating_words.locating_word: release aerosol sprays into the stratosphere

target_sen.target_sen: This would involve using sulphur or hydrogen sulphide aerosols so that sulphur dioxide would form clouds, which would, in turn, lead to a global dimming.

locating_words.locating_word: reduce the amount of light reaching Earth

questions.analysis: 答案是“cables”，因为题干中的“fix strong 【 】to Greenland ice sheets”与原文中的“preserve the ice sheets of Greenland with reinforced high-tension cables”相对应。题干中的“fix”对应“preserve”，“strong”对应“reinforced”，“Greenland ice sheets”对应“ice sheets of Greenland”。在这句话中，"cables" 是主要名词，符合题干中需要填入的词性。

central_sentences.sentence: Scientists have also scrutinised whether it's possible to preserve the ice sheets of Greenland with reinforced high-tension cables, preventing icebergs from moving into the sea.
central_sentences.translation: 科学家们还研究了是否可以使用加固的高张力缆线来保护格陵兰冰盖，防止冰山移入海中。

target_sen.target_sen: Scientists have also scrutinised whether it's possible to preserve the ice sheets of Greenland with reinforced high-tension cables, preventing icebergs from moving into the sea.
Scientists have also scrutinised whether it's possible to preserve the ice sheets of Greenland with reinforced high-tension cables, preventing icebergs from moving into the sea.

questions.analysis: 答案是“snow”，因为文章中明确提到“birches would shed their leaves in winter, thus enabling radiation to be reflected by the snow”，即桦树在冬天落叶后，雪能够反射辐射。题干中的“plant trees in Russian Arctic that would lose their leaves in winter”对应文章中的“planting of millions of birch trees”和“shed their leaves in winter”，而“to allow the 【 】to reflect radiation”对应文章中的“enabling radiation to be reflected by the snow”。因此，正确答案是“snow”。

central_sentences.sentence: Meanwhile in the Russian Arctic, geo-engineering plans include the planting of millions of birch trees. Whereas the region's native evergreen pines shade the snow and absorb radiation, birches would shed their leaves in winter, thus enabling radiation to be reflected by the snow.
central_sentences.translation: 与此同时，在俄罗斯北极地区，地球工程计划包括种植数百万棵桦树。虽然该地区的原生常绿松树会遮蔽雪地并吸收辐射，但桦树会在冬季落叶，从而使雪能够反射辐射。

syn_ary.synonym: planting of millions of birch trees

target_sen.target_sen: Meanwhile in the Russian Arctic, geo-engineering plans include the planting of millions of birch trees.
Whereas the region's native evergreen pines shade the snow and absorb radiation, birches would shed their leaves in winter, thus enabling radiation to be reflected by the snow.
Whereas the region's native evergreen pines shade the snow and absorb radiation, birches would shed their leaves in winter, thus enabling radiation to be reflected by the snow.

questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词是“change the direction of”，意思是“改变方向”。在原文中，最直接对应的同义替换词是“Re-routing”，意思是“重新引导、改变方向”。原文中提到“Re-routing Russian rivers”，即重新引导俄罗斯的河流，这与题干中的描述完全一致。因此，正确答案是“rivers”。

central_sentences.sentence: Re-routing Russian rivers to increase cold water flow to ice-forming areas could also be used to slow down warming, say some climate scientists.
central_sentences.translation: 一些气候科学家表示，重新引导俄罗斯河流以增加流向结冰区域的冷水量，也可以用来减缓全球变暖。

target_sen.target_sen: Re-routing Russian rivers to increase cold water flow to ice-forming areas could also be used to slow down warming, say some climate scientists.

groups.title: List of Scientists
groups.desc:
Questions 37-40
Look at the following statements (Questions 37-40) and the list of scientists below. 
Match each statement with the correct scientist, A-D.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

questions.title: The effects of geo-engineering may not be long-lasting.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B. Phil Rasch。题干中的“effects of geo-engineering may not be long-lasting”在原文中有对应的表达：“if we were to end geo-engineering on a given day, then the planet would return to its pre-engineered condition very rapidly, and probably within ten to twenty years.” 这句话明确表示了地球工程的效果不会持久，符合题干的意思。

central_sentences.sentence: I think all of us agree that if we were to end geo-engineering on a given day, then the planet would return to its pre-engineered condition very rapidly, and probably within ten to twenty years. That's certainly something to worry about.
central_sentences.translation: 我认为我们都同意，如果我们在某一天停止地球工程，那么地球将会很快恢复到工程前的状态，可能在十到二十年内。这确实是一个值得担心的问题。

syn_ary.synonym: planet would return to its pre-engineered condition

target_sen.target_sen: And Dr Phil Rasch of the US-based Pacific Northwest National Laboratory is equally guarded about the role of geo-engineering: 'I think all of us agree that if we were to end geo-engineering on a given day, then the planet would return to its pre-engineered condition very rapidly, and probably within ten to twenty years.

And Dr Phil Rasch of the US-based Pacific Northwest National Laboratory is equally guarded about the role of geo-engineering: 'I think all of us agree that if we were to end geo-engineering on a given day, then the planet would return to its pre-engineered condition very rapidly, and probably within ten to twenty years.

And Dr Phil Rasch of the US-based Pacific Northwest National Laboratory is equally guarded about the role of geo-engineering: 'I think all of us agree that if we were to end geo-engineering on a given day, then the planet would return to its pre-engineered condition very rapidly, and probably within ten to twenty years.

questions.title: Geo-engineering is a topic worth exploring.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D. Martin Sommerkorn，因为他在文章中明确表示了对地球工程这个话题进行深入思考和探索的必要性。这与题干“Geo-engineering is a topic worth exploring”直接对应。通过关键词定位和同义词识别，即使词汇量较差的学生也能找到正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: According to Dr Martin Sommerkorn, climate change advisor for the World Wildlife Fund's International Arctic Programme, 'Human-induced climate change has brought humanity to a position where we shouldn't exclude thinking thoroughly about this topic and its possibilities.'
central_sentences.translation: 根据世界野生动物基金会国际北极项目气候变化顾问Martin Sommerkorn博士的说法，"人为导致的气候变化已经使人类处于不应该排除深入思考这个话题及其可能性的境地。"

target_sen.target_sen: The main reason why geo-engineering is supported by many in the scientific community is that most researchers have little faith in the ability of politicians to agree - and then bring in - the necessary carbon cuts.
Even leading conservation organisations see the value of investigating the potential of geo-engineering.
Even leading conservation organisations see the value of investigating the potential of geo-engineering.

syn_ary.synonym: thinking thoroughly about this topic and its possibilities

target_sen.target_sen: According to Dr Martin Sommerkorn, climate change advisor for the World Wildlife Fund's International Arctic Programme, 'Human-induced climate change has brought humanity to a position where we shouldn't exclude thinking thoroughly about this topic and its possibilities.'

questions.title: It may be necessary to limit the effectiveness of geo-engineering projects.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C（Dan Lunt），因为他在文章中提到，为了避免地球工程项目可能带来的“超调”风险，Angel的项目必须以半强度运行，这与题干中的“limit the effectiveness”相对应。

central_sentences.sentence: To avoid such a scenario, Lunt says Angel's project would have to operate at half strength; all of which reinforces his view that the best option is to avoid the need for geo-engineering altogether.
central_sentences.translation: 为了避免这种情况，Lunt说Angel的项目必须以半强度运行；所有这些都强化了他的观点，即最好的选择是完全避免地球工程的需要。

target_sen.target_sen: 'To avoid such a scenario, Lunt says Angel's project would have to operate at half strength; all of which reinforces his view that the best option is to avoid the need for geo-engineering altogether.

questions.title: Research into non-fossil-based fuels cannot be replaced by geo-engineering.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“non-fossil-based fuels”在原文中对应的是“renewable energy”，“geo-engineering”在原文中也是“geo-engineering”，“cannot be replaced”在原文中对应的是“no substitute for”。这些同义替换词在原文中都能找到，且明确表示了地球工程不能替代开发可再生能源。因此，答案是 Roger Angel。

central_sentences.sentence: Angel says that his plan is 'no substitute for developing renewable energy: the only permanent solution'.
central_sentences.translation: 安吉尔说他的计划"不能替代开发可再生能源：这才是唯一的永久解决方案"。

target_sen.target_sen: Generally speaking, those who are most cautious about geo-engineering are the scientists involved in the research.

And Dr Phil Rasch of the US-based Pacific Northwest National Laboratory is equally guarded about the role of geo-engineering: 'I think all of us agree that if we were to end geo-engineering on a given day, then the planet would return to its pre-engineered condition very rapidly, and probably within ten to twenty years.
Angel says that his plan is 'no substitute for developing renewable energy: the only permanent solution'.
